questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{5} - 1) / (x^{7} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (5x^(4)) / (7x^(6)). At x=1, this is 5/7.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(6x) - sin(4x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(6x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(4x)/x = 6 - 4 = 2.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{3} - 1) / (x^{7} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (3x^(2)) / (7x^(6)). At x=1, this is 3/7.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(11x) - sin(7x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(11x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(7x)/x = 11 - 7 = 4.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(12x) - sin(4x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(12x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(4x)/x = 12 - 4 = 8.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+1)/(x-5))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{10x} - e^{8x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+6)/(x-4))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{5} - 1) / (x^{6} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (5x^(4)) / (6x^(5)). At x=1, this is 5/6.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{11x} - e^{9x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(10x) - sin(3x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(10x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(3x)/x = 10 - 3 = 7.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+3)/(x-2))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(12x) - sin(6x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(12x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(6x)/x = 12 - 6 = 6.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(9x) - sin(2x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(9x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(2x)/x = 9 - 2 = 7.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+2)/(x-5))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{12x} - e^{2x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(9x) - sin(7x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(9x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(7x)/x = 9 - 7 = 2.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+2)/(x-3))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{2} - 1) / (x^{5} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (2x^(1)) / (5x^(4)). At x=1, this is 2/5.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+4)/(x-2))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.
